hero.badge: Real zero-knowledge · Encrypted on your device · Open source
hero.titleLine2: Encrypted before it leaves your device.
hero.subtitle: Most clouds promise not to look at your files. Noctcom is built so that it can't, even if we wanted to. Your password and your keys never touch our servers. We only store encrypted data we can't open.

zk.title: Zero-knowledge by default
zk.body: Not a setting you turn on: it's the only way it works. Argon2id + XChaCha20-Poly1305.

metadata.title: Not even metadata
metadata.body: Names, sizes and tags are encrypted too. Not just the content.

email.title: No email in the clear
email.body: We don't even store your email: just a hash. A database dump won't reveal who you are.

share.title: Anonymous sharing
share.body: X25519 sealed boxes: only the recipient opens the file. Not us, not anyone with server access.

demo.title: Don't take our word for it: <hl>watch it</hl>.
demo.subtitle: The full security walkthrough in 84 seconds: what happens to your password, what travels over the network, and what our own server can (and can't) see.

notDo.title: What we <hl>don't</hl> do.
notDo.body: Noctcom is not an office suite. There's no chat, no video calls, no online document editor. It's deliberate: every feature that gives a server the ability to read your data is a crack in your privacy. We do one thing —store your files so that only you can read them— and we do it better than anyone.
notDo.note: Algorithm versioning ready for post-quantum (Kyber/Dilithium on the roadmap). Your encryption today won't expire tomorrow.

audience.title: For those to whom privacy <hl>isn't optional</hl>.
audience.subtitle: If you handle material that can't leak —an investigation, a source, a case file— you need more than a provider's promise to «not look». You need it to be unable to.

journalism.title: Investigative journalism
journalism.body: Keep sensitive material encrypted on your device before it ever leaves it. Not even we can open it.

sources.title: Sources and whistleblowing
sources.body: Share with your newsroom via X25519 sealed boxes: only the recipient opens it, not even with server access.

professional.title: Professional confidentiality
professional.body: Lawyers, doctors, journalists: client, patient or source data with encryption the provider can't break.

jurisdiction.title: Control and jurisdiction
jurisdiction.body: AGPL self-host: your data on your own hardware, outside other people's jurisdictions and providers.

audience.roadmapNote: On the roadmap — verifiable chain of custody: authorship signing and timestamping to prove to an editor or a judge that a file is intact and from a given date, without anyone else reading it. All built on hashes and signatures; the content never leaves your control.
audience.honestyNote: Honesty first: zero-knowledge encryption protects your files at rest, it doesn't give you network anonymity. To hide who you talk to, combine it with tools like Tor according to your threat model. And like everything in Noctcom, the code is open and auditable.

selfhost.title: Your server, your rules.
selfhost.body: Noctcom is 100% open source (AGPL-3.0). Deploy it on your own hardware with a single command: Docker on any Linux, or an LXC on Proxmox VE. Same encryption, no third parties. Your cloud, your hardware, real zero-knowledge.
selfhost.readyComment: # With a domain: automatic TLS · without one: http://<IP> on your LAN

control.title: Total control
control.body: Your data on your infrastructure. No middlemen, no external jurisdictions.

auditable.title: Auditable
auditable.body: Open source AGPL-3.0. Verify every line that protects your files.

sameCrypto.title: Same encryption
sameCrypto.body: Identical cryptographic guarantees as the cloud version. Real zero-knowledge.

about.badge: Redder Labs · Independent · Privacy by design
about.title: Behind Noctcom there's <hl>one person</hl>, not a corporation.
about.intro: Noctcom is built by Julián Rodríguez, a self-taught developer, under the Redder Labs brand. No investors, no marketing team, no meetings. Just code and one fixed idea: that your data shouldn't be the product.

bio.title: How I got here
bio.p1: I started programming at 7, building computers on my own. At 16 I made my first websites. Everything else came the same way: reading other people's code and breaking things until I understood why they broke.
bio.quote: «Everything I know I learned by reading other people's code, breaking things until I understood why they broke.»
bio.p2: I work alone, on my own hours, and I self-fund. That shapes the product: nothing depends on selling your attention or your data, because there's no one to answer to other than the people who use it.

philosophy.title: Why Noctcom works this way
philosophy.p1: Every tool I make shares one principle: the processing happens on your device, not on a server that watches you. In Xero Trace that means your location stays private, not a product. In Noctcom it means your files are encrypted before they leave your machine, and all the server ever receives is something it can't open.
philosophy.p2: It's not an option you turn on: it's the only way it's built. And like everything I make, the code is open (AGPL-3.0) so you don't have to take my word for it: you can check it line by line.

projects.title: Other Redder Labs projects
projects.xeroTrace: A personal safety app that detects falls and accidents by analyzing the accelerometer, processing everything on the device. «When seconds matter, not recordings».

contact.title: Let's talk
contact.security: Found a security flaw? Report it responsibly by following <link>SECURITY.md</link>.

cta.title: Try the idea, not the promise
cta.subtitle: 1 GB free with zero-knowledge encryption. No card, no tricks.

header.title: Security and cryptography
header.intro: We don't ask you to take our word for it. Noctcom is built so that, even if we wanted to, we can't read your files: your keys are born and stay on your device, and the server only ever receives ciphertext it has no way to open. Here we explain exactly how, no spin. And if anything on this page doesn't match the code, open an issue — in the end the code rules, not the promises.

keyHierarchy.title: Key hierarchy
keyHierarchy.commentRecovery: Parallel branch: the recovery phrase (12 BIP39 words)

recovery.title: Account recovery (kit v2)
recovery.intro: Your 12-word phrase (BIP39, 128 bits of entropy) doesn't just recover access: it also recovers your files. From it a X25519 pair is derived whose public key is registered on the server; with that public key, your browser seals (crypto_box_seal) each vault's key and your exchange key. Sealing only needs the public key — opening the seals needs only the private key, which exists solely if you have the phrase.

forgotPassword.title: You forgot the password → nothing is lost
forgotPassword.text: The phrase signs a challenge, the server hands over the seals, your browser opens them and re-encrypts the vault keys with your new password. Your files and everything shared with you stay with you.

sealedEnvelopes.title: The server only keeps sealed envelopes
sealedEnvelopes.text: The seals are ciphertext: neither Noctcom nor an attacker with the database can open them. The private key that opens them is derived from your phrase and never travels.

lostBoth.title: You lose phrase AND password → unrecoverable
lostBoth.text: There's no back door, no 'contact support'. It's the price of real zero-knowledge, and we say it with no fine print.

chunks.title: 4 MiB chunks
chunks.text: Each file is split into 4 MiB chunks, encrypted independently with XChaCha20-Poly1305. Each chunk uses a random 24-byte nonce.

aad.title: Anti-reorder AAD
aad.text: Each chunk includes its index as Additional Authenticated Data (AAD = 'chunk:N'). It prevents an attacker from reordering chunks.

contentHash.title: Content hash
contentHash.text: BLAKE2b-256 over all encrypted chunks. Verifies integrity on every download.

zkEmail.title: Zero-knowledge email
zkEmail.comment: The server never sees your email

audits.title: External audits
audits.body: No external audits have been carried out yet. Once they are completed, the full reports will be published here and in the repository.
audits.note: In the meantime, the code is 100% auditable on <link>GitHub</link>.

A1.name: Malicious operator
A1.desc: Noctcom employee with access to infrastructure

A2.name: Database dump
A2.desc: Breach, court order, or operational error

A3.name: Active MITM
A3.desc: Malicious ISP, compromised WiFi, BGP hijacking

A5.name: Compromised client
A5.desc: Malware, keylogger, RAT on your device

notProtected.title: What we do NOT protect against

items.malware: Malware or a keylogger on your device
items.physicalAccess: Physical access to your unlocked device
items.networkAnonymity: Network anonymity (use Tor if you need it)

assumptions.title: Cryptographic assumptions
assumptions.intro: If any of these primitives is broken (e.g. real quantum computing), we will migrate to post-quantum primitives (Kyber, Dilithium) via re-encryption + key rotation.

footerCta.title: Audit the code yourself
footerCta.body: All the code is public under AGPL-3.0. The reference implementations are in <code1></code1> and <code2></code2>

roadmap.heading: Where Noctcom is headed
roadmap.intro: What already works, what we're finishing up and what comes next. No promised dates that later go unmet: the core —encryption, accounts, files, sharing— is already standing and solid, and everything else builds on top of it. All the progress lives in the open, so the most reliable version of this list is always the code itself.

crypto-core.title: Cryptographic core
crypto-core.desc: Argon2id, XChaCha20-Poly1305, Ed25519, X25519 and BLAKE2b, with the same implementation in the browser and on the server.

accounts-access.title: Accounts and access
accounts-access.desc: Sign-up with a mnemonic phrase (BIP39), login via Ed25519 challenge-response and two-step verification: passkeys (fingerprint or face) and email code.

encrypted-files.title: Your files, encrypted
encrypted-files.desc: Upload and download in 4 MiB chunks, a key per file, trash and versions. The server only ever sees ciphertext.

file-preview.title: File preview
file-preview.desc: View images, video, audio, text and PDF decrypted in memory, without having to download them.

e2e-sharing.title: End-to-end sharing with contacts
e2e-sharing.desc: You add someone as a contact (they have to accept — nobody can push files to you unasked), then share files sealed with their public key (X25519). The recipient downloads and decrypts on their device; not even we can open what's passed around.

multi-device.title: Multiple devices
multi-device.desc: You register and revoke devices, and your keys sync from your password without passing through the server in the clear.

realtime-sync.title: Instant changes
realtime-sync.desc: What you touch in one place shows up everywhere else right away, across devices and across tabs.

account-recovery.title: Account recovery
account-recovery.desc: With your 12-word phrase you recover access and also your files, re-encrypted with your new password.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.desc: Alerts when someone shares something with you, even if you have the app closed. The alert never reveals the content.

own-disks.title: Your own disks
own-disks.desc: Beyond the cloud, you can use physical disks (USB/SATA) as storage and choose where each thing lives.

ops-reliability.title: Operations and reliability
ops-reliability.desc: Automatic TLS, rate limits, error monitoring, verified backups, health checks and a guide to host it yourself.

legal-privacy.title: Legal notice and privacy
legal-privacy.desc: Terms, privacy policy (GDPR) and cookie policy, with no third-party trackers.

storage-plans.title: Storage plans
storage-plans.desc: Start free with 1 GB. Monthly plans to add more space are nearly ready; self-hosting is always free.

independent-audit.title: Independent audit
independent-audit.desc: A security review and a pentest by outside people, once we can afford it.

evidence-mode.title: Evidence mode and chain of custody
evidence-mode.desc: Prove that a file is intact and what date it's from, with timestamping, without anyone seeing its content. Designed for journalism, whistleblowing and the legal field.

mobile-app.title: Installable mobile app
mobile-app.desc: Install Noctcom on your phone or tablet (Android and iOS) to upload and access your encrypted files.

desktop-app.title: Desktop app
desktop-app.desc: A single application that brings together your vault and the management of your disks, without depending on the browser.

light-dark-theme.title: Light and dark theme
light-dark-theme.desc: Choose the look you like best, with accessibility improvements.

i18n.title: Spanish and English
i18n.desc: The whole website and app in two languages, with their own per-language URLs. Switch between Spanish and English whenever you want.

cta.title: Missing something?
cta.desc: Ideas and bugs are discussed in the open. If you want to propose something or see the fine detail of each phase, drop by the repository.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: Short version: we do not use tracking cookies, neither first-party nor third-party, nor analytics that profile you. Here is the full detail, for transparency.

necessary.title: 1. Strictly necessary cookies
necessary.p1: The only cookies set are those from Cloudflare, our network provider, to protect the service against attacks and malicious traffic:
necessary.item1: __cf_bm — distinguishes human traffic from bots (bot management).
necessary.item2: cf_clearance — remembers that you have passed a security check.
necessary.p2: They are strictly necessary for the security of the service, do not collect personal data for marketing purposes and, in accordance with the regulations, do not require prior consent.

notUsed.title: 2. What we do NOT use
notUsed.p1: We do not use Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, advertising pixels or third-party tracking cookies. We do not build profiles or share your browsing with advertisers. If we were to add analytics in the future, it would be privacy-respecting and cookieless, and it would be reflected here.

localStorage.title: 3. Local storage in your browser
localStorage.p1: These are not cookies, but for transparency: Noctcom uses your browser's local storage to function, and that data never leaves your device nor is it shared:
localStorage.item1: sessionStorage: the cryptographic material of your session (keys in memory). It is cleared when you close the tab.
localStorage.item2: localStorage: preferences such as the device identifier, whether you accepted this notice or whether you enabled notifications.

manage.title: 4. How to manage them
manage.p1: You can delete or block cookies from your browser settings. Please note that blocking Cloudflare's security cookies may prevent access to the service.

hero.title:
You pay for space,

never for your data.
hero.subtitle: The zero-knowledge encryption is identical on every plan. Only how much you store changes. Start free with 1 GB; scale up when you need to. Cancel whenever you want.

pricing.startFree: Every account starts with 1 GB free. Scale up when you need to.
pricing.billingSoon: Paid plans go live shortly. In the meantime, start free with 1 GB.
pricing.billingNote: Monthly billing · No commitment · Taxes apply depending on your country

trust.intro: Your keys never leave your device. All the code is public and verifiable.

included.title: Included on <emph>every</emph> plan
included.subtitle: Privacy isn't an add-on you pay for separately. It's the foundation, the same for everyone.

items.share: Encrypted sharing: only the recipient can open it
items.export: Export your entire vault whenever you want

specs.title: Technical specifications
specs.subtitle: The same cryptography, whatever you pay.

selfHost.title: Prefer your own server? It's free.
selfHost.body: Noctcom is open source (AGPL-3.0). Deploy it on your own hardware with a single Docker command: same encryption, unlimited capacity, zero cost. Your cloud, your rules.

plan-change.q: Can I change plans whenever I want?
plan-change.a: Yes, instantly. Stripe prorates the difference automatically, with no penalties.

cancel.a: You go back to the free 1 GB plan. We don't delete anything: you keep your files and can download or export them. If you exceed the free quota, the account becomes read-only until you free up space.

space.q: Is the space for my files or for the encryption?
space.a: We measure the encrypted size stored, which is practically the same as the original (encryption adds just a few bytes per block).

payment.a: Payment is handled by Stripe from start to finish; Noctcom never sees your card details. And as always, your files remain unreadable to us.

self-host.q: What if I prefer my own server?
self-host.a: Self-hosting is free forever under AGPL-3.0: same encryption, unlimited capacity, zero cost. You only pay if you use our managed infrastructure.

cta.title: Start today with <emph>1 GB free</emph>.
cta.subtitle: No card, no tricks. Encrypted on your device from the very first file.
cta.legal: By creating an account you accept the <terms>Terms</terms> and the <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These terms govern the use of Noctcom. By creating an account or using the service, you accept what is described herein. They are written to be understood: if something does not work for you, write to us at <link>hello@noctcom.com</link>.

provider.title: 1. Who provides the service
provider.p1: Noctcom is a zero-knowledge encrypted storage service operated by Redder Labs, an independent development project (hereinafter, "we"). Contact: hello@noctcom.com.
provider.p2: Noctcom's software is open source under the AGPL-3.0 license. You may audit it, modify it, and self-host it under that license. These terms govern the managed service we offer at noctcom.com; self-hosting is governed by the AGPL-3.0, not by this document.

what.title: 2. What Noctcom is (and is not)
what.p1: Noctcom encrypts your files on your device before uploading them. Our servers store only encrypted data that we cannot decrypt. We have no access to the content, to your file names, or to your keys.
what.p2: Noctcom is a storage service, not an office productivity suite or a real-time communication tool. We do not analyze, index, or monetize your content — among other reasons because, by design, we cannot.

account.title: 3. Your account, your password, and your recovery phrase
account.p1: The security of your account depends on your master password and your 12-word recovery phrase. You are responsible for safeguarding them.
account.warning: Critical warning: due to the zero-knowledge nature of the service, if you lose your password and your recovery phrase, your data is unrecoverable. There is no backdoor, no reset through support, and no copy of your keys in our possession. Be sure to keep your recovery phrase in a safe place.

acceptableUse.title: 4. Acceptable use
acceptableUse.p1: You agree not to use Noctcom to store or distribute unlawful content, nor for activities that infringe the law or the rights of third parties. As we cannot see your content, responsibility for what you store rests entirely with you.
acceptableUse.p2: We do not monitor content (we cannot), but we may act upon valid legal notices or signs of service abuse (e.g., automated use to overload the infrastructure), suspending accounts where appropriate.

yourFiles.title: 5. Your files are yours
yourFiles.p1: You retain all rights over the content you upload. We acquire no ownership or license over your files beyond the technical (encrypted) storage necessary to provide you with the service. You may export your entire vault at any time.

plans.title: 6. Plans and pricing
plans.p1: The managed service includes an initial free quota. Paid plans, when activated, charge exclusively for storage capacity (bytes), never for your content. Self-hosting is and will always be free under AGPL-3.0. Any price changes will be communicated with reasonable advance notice.

availability.title: 7. Availability and "as is"
availability.p1: Noctcom is provided "as is" and "as available". We work to keep the service operational, but we do not guarantee uninterrupted uptime. There may be maintenance, outages, or changes. In this early stage, we recommend keeping your own copies of critical data.

liability.title: 8. Limitation of liability
liability.p1: To the maximum extent permitted by law, we shall not be liable for data loss arising from the loss of your keys (password/phrase), nor for indirect or consequential damages. Given the zero-knowledge design, we cannot recover encrypted data without your keys, and you accept that risk by using the service.

cancellation.title: 9. Cancellation
cancellation.p1: You may delete your account and your data at any time from the settings. We may suspend or close accounts that breach these terms or pose a risk to the service or to third parties.

changes.title: 10. Changes to the terms
changes.p1: We may update these terms. If the changes are substantial, we will communicate them through available means (e.g., a notice in the app or a service email). Continued use after they take effect constitutes acceptance.

law.title: 11. Governing law
law.p1: These terms are governed by Spanish and European Union law. The processing of personal data is detailed in the <link>Privacy Policy</link>.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Privacy is not a section in Noctcom: it is the architecture. The server is built so that it cannot read your content. This policy explains what data we process, why, for how long, and what rights you have (GDPR).

controller.title: 1. Data controller
controller.p1: Redder Labs, the controller for the processing of data in the Noctcom service. For any privacy matter or to exercise your rights: hello@noctcom.com.

zeroKnowledge.title: 2. The zero-knowledge principle
zeroKnowledge.p1: Your files are encrypted on your device with keys derived from your password, which never leaves it. What reaches our servers is ciphertext that we cannot decrypt: neither the content, nor the file names, nor the metadata. It is not a promise of "not looking": it is a technical impossibility, verifiable in the open source code (AGPL-3.0).

data.title: 3. What data we process
data.intro: We process the bare minimum necessary for the service to function:
data.item1: Hash of the email address (BLAKE2b). We do not store your email in plaintext. When we need to send you an email (verification, access code, recovery), your client sends us the email solely for that delivery and it is transmitted to our email provider; it is not stored in our database.
data.item2: Username that you choose (it may be a pseudonym).
data.item3: Encrypted content: file blobs, names and metadata, all end-to-end encrypted. To us they are opaque bytes.
data.item4: Technical security data: session and access-attempt logs with the hashed IP address (not in plaintext), to prevent abuse and brute force.
data.item5: Device information (e.g. the browser), stored encrypted so that you recognise your sessions.
data.item6: Error reports: in the event of an application failure, the technical error is logged (stack trace, browser) so that it can be fixed; this may include the IP address of the request. It does not include your content.
data.outro: We do not create advertising profiles, we do not sell data, and we do not use tracking cookies (see the Cookie Policy).

purpose.title: 4. Purposes and legal bases
purpose.item1: Providing the service (account, storage, synchronisation): performance of the contract (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR).
purpose.item2: Security and abuse prevention (IP hashes, rate limits, logs): legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)).
purpose.item3: Error correction (technical reports): legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)), minimising data.
purpose.item4: Service communications (verification, codes, notices): performance of the contract.

processors.title: 5. Processors and international transfers
processors.p1: To operate, we use providers that act as data processors. They handle only encrypted data or the minimum necessary:
processors.item3: Backblaze B2 — storage of the encrypted blobs.
processors.item4: Resend — email delivery (receives your email only to deliver the message).
processors.item5: Google / Firebase — push notifications (if you enable them).
processors.item7: Cloudflare — distribution network and protection against attacks.
processors.outro: Some providers may process data outside the European Economic Area (e.g. the USA). In such cases, the transfers are covered by the safeguards provided for under the GDPR (standard contractual clauses or applicable adequacy frameworks). Given the zero-knowledge design, what these providers store is, for the most part, encrypted and unreadable to them.

retention.title: 6. How long we keep the data
retention.item1: Account and encrypted content: for as long as your account exists. When you delete it, they are removed.
retention.item3: Sessions: until they expire or are revoked.
retention.item4: Backups: retained for a limited window and rotated; the content remains encrypted.

rights.title: 7. Your rights (GDPR)
rights.intro: You may exercise at any time your rights of:
rights.item2: Erasure: you can delete your account and data from the settings.
rights.item3: Portability: export your entire vault whenever you wish.
rights.outro: To exercise them, write to us at <link>hello@noctcom.com</link>. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the competent supervisory authority (in Spain, the <authority>Spanish Data Protection Agency</authority>).
rights.note: Note: due to zero-knowledge encryption, we cannot access the content of your account to handle a request about specific files; you retain that access with your keys.

minors.title: 8. Minors
minors.p1: Noctcom is not directed at children under 14 years of age. If you believe that a minor has provided us with data without the appropriate consent, contact us and we will resolve it.

changes.title: 9. Changes to this policy
changes.p1: We may update this policy. Substantial changes will be communicated through the available channels. The "last revised" date above indicates the version in force.

cookieBanner.title: Cookies
cookieBanner.description: Noctcom does not use tracking cookies. The only active cookies are those set by Cloudflare for DDoS protection and network security (, ): strictly necessary and they do not collect personal data. Learn more.

twoFactor.title: Two-step verification
twoFactor.subtitle: Your password is correct. Confirm your identity to unlock.
twoFactor.sendCodeEmail: Send a code to my email

errors.locked: Too many failed attempts. Account temporarily locked — try again in {minutes, plural, one {# minute} other {# minutes}}.

form.title: Create account
form.subtitle: Your password will never be sent to our servers
form.emailHint: We only store a hash. You'll never get spam.
form.important: Important: If you forget this password and lose your recovery phrase, your data will be unrecoverable. We have no way to restore it.
form.legal: By creating an account you accept the <terms>Terms</terms> and the <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.

mnemonic.title: Your recovery phrase
mnemonic.subtitle: Keep it somewhere safe. It's your only way to recover your account.
mnemonic.confirmCheckbox: I've saved my recovery phrase somewhere safe and I understand it's the only way to recover my account.

confirm.title: Almost done
confirm.subtitle: We'll confirm a few random words

errors.usernameTooShort: The username must have at least 3 valid characters.
errors.passwordTooWeak: Your password is too weak. Use at least 12 characters with numbers and symbols.
errors.wordsMismatch: The words don't match. Check your recovery phrase.

toasts.copied: Copied to clipboard (it'll be cleared in 60s)
toasts.accountCreated: Account created — check your email to verify
toasts.tooManySignups: Too many accounts have been created from this connection. Try again in a few days.

toast.phraseMismatch: The phrase doesn't match this account. Check the words and their order.
toast.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters

subtitle.mnemonic: In the exact order you generated it
subtitle.newPassword: Your vault will be re-encrypted with this password
subtitle.doneKit: Your keys and your files stay with you
subtitle.doneNoKit: Your keys have been regenerated with the new password

email.warning: Recovering your account revokes all active sessions. If your account has the full recovery kit, your files and shares are preserved.
email.label: Email

mnemonic.pasteHint: Paste the full phrase into any field to fill them all automatically

newPassword.kitVerified: Recovery kit verified. Your vaults{count} will be re-encrypted with the new password: files and shares stay accessible.
newPassword.noKit: This account doesn't have the full recovery kit. You'll regain access, but files encrypted with your previous password can't be decrypted. After signing in, enable the kit in Settings → Security so it doesn't happen again.
newPassword.label: New master password

done.messageKit: Your account has been restored: your vaults were re-encrypted with the new password and your files stay accessible. All previous sessions have been revoked.
done.messageNoKit: Your account has been restored and your keys regenerated with the new password. All previous sessions have been revoked.

verify.title: Verify your email
verify.subtitle: We sent you a 6-digit code. Check your inbox.
verify.emailNotStored: Your email is not stored on our servers

toast.emailRequired: Enter your email to resend the code

welcome.title: Welcome to your vault
welcome.body:
Everything you upload is encrypted on this device, with your password, before it ever leaves it. Only locked boxes reach our servers: we cannot read your files — not even their names.

It isn't a promise to behave — it's built so that looking is impossible.

phrase.title: Your 12-word phrase
phrase.body: The recovery phrase you saved when signing up is the only way back in if you forget your password.
phrase.hint: There is no "reset by email": we keep no copy of your password or your phrase. Store it away from this device (on paper, for example). You can verify it anytime in Settings → Recovery kit.

upload.title: Upload your first file
upload.body: Use the "New" button in the sidebar or drag files into the vault. Each file is chunked and encrypted in your browser before uploading — you'll see encryption and upload progress in real time.

security.title: Extra protection (optional)
security.body:
You can add a second factor to your login: a passkey (fingerprint, Windows Hello…) or an email code. Enable it in Settings → Security.

You can also review and revoke devices with open sessions.

ready.title: All set
ready.body:
You have 1 GB free to get started. If anything is unclear, the manual is always in the sidebar.

Your files, your keys, your vault.

trialWelcome.title: Your trial period just started
trialWelcome.body:
You have {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}} with 10 GB to try the Noctcom beta: upload files, explore your vault and settings, and tell us how it goes.

Any paid plan (from €1/month) also unlocks Noctcom Connector, to link your hard drives or USB sticks as your own storage.
trialWelcome.hint: The sidebar shows a countdown with the days left. When it ends, your account stays free with 1 GB forever: nothing gets deleted, but if you use more than 1 GB you'll only be able to download or free up space until you pick a plan.

trialEnded.title: Your trial period has ended
trialEnded.body:
Your account is still free, now with 1 GB of space. Nothing gets deleted: your files are still here and you can download them anytime.

If you're using more than 1 GB, you won't be able to upload anything new until you free up space or upgrade.
trialEnded.hint: Any plan (from €1/month) gets you more space back and unlocks Noctcom Connector to link your hard drives or USB sticks.

empty.title: Nothing here yet
empty.description: Drag files into this window or create a folder to get started

pagination.status: Page {page} of {total} · {count, plural, one {# item} other {# items}}

date.daysAgo: {count, plural, one {# day ago} other {# days ago}}

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Security, devices and preferences for your vault

plan.title: Plan & usage
plan.revertsToFree: Reverts to the Free plan on {date}
plan.nearLimit: You're close to your plan limit.
plan.expandHint: Add space whenever you need it.

planPicker.title: Choose your plan
planPicker.subtitle: Same zero-knowledge encryption. Only the space changes. Switch or cancel whenever you want.
planPicker.securePayment: Secure payment with Stripe · Noctcom never sees your card

password.cardDesc: Re-encrypts all your keys with a new password. Your files are not re-uploaded.
password.modalDesc: Your keys are re-encrypted locally. The server never sees either password.
password.tooShort: The new password must be at least 8 characters
password.mustDiffer: The new password must be different from the current one
password.changed: Master password changed. Sessions on other devices have been closed.

passkeys.title: Passkeys (WebAuthn)
passkeys.desc: Use your fingerprint, Face ID or a physical security key as a second factor. The private key never leaves your device.
passkeys.empty: You don't have any passkey registered yet.

emailOtp.title: Email code
emailOtp.descVerified: We'll ask for a 6-digit code sent to your email when you sign in.
emailOtp.descUnverified: Verify your email first to enable this option.

recovery.title: Recovery kit
recovery.checkingDesc: Checking the status of your recovery kit.
recovery.completeDesc: If you recover the account with your phrase, {count, plural, one {your files} other {your # vaults}} and shares are preserved.
recovery.incompleteDesc: With your phrase you'd regain access, but not your files. Re-enter your phrase (or generate a new one) to complete it.
recovery.enterInstructions: Enter your 12-word recovery phrase. It's verified locally: it never leaves your browser.
recovery.generateWarning: This phrase replaces the previous one, which will stop working. Keep it somewhere safe: it's your only way in if you forget your password.
recovery.copiedClipboard: Copied to clipboard (it will be cleared in 60s)
recovery.savedConfirmLabel: I've saved the new phrase somewhere safe and I understand the previous one will stop working.
recovery.noVaultsLoaded: No vaults loaded — open your vault and try again
recovery.phraseMismatch: That phrase doesn't belong to this account
recovery.newPhraseActivated: New phrase activated — the previous one no longer works

push.title: Notifications
push.cardTitle: Alerts when files are shared with you
push.unsupported: This browser doesn't support push notifications.
push.denied: Blocked in the browser — enable them from the lock icon in the address bar.
push.activeDesc: You'll get an alert even when the tab is closed. The alert content is generic: your file names stay encrypted.
push.inactiveDesc: Enable alerts in this browser. Only the event is notified — never the content, which is encrypted.
push.deniedToast: The browser has notifications blocked for this site

devices.title: Active devices
devices.noOthers: No other devices with an active session.

storage.title: Storage
storage.emptyBreakdown: The breakdown by file type will appear once you upload files.

connector.intro: Install the agent on your computer to manage its disks from here. It opens an encrypted outbound connection (no open ports) and your keys never leave your machine.
connector.step1Windows: We detected Windows. Download the executable and open it.
connector.step1OtherOs: We detected {os}: its binary is coming soon. For now there's a Windows version.
connector.step1Generic: Download the agent for your system.
connector.buildsSoon: Builds for macOS and Linux: coming soon.
connector.step2: 2 · Open a terminal in the folder where it was downloaded (usually Downloads) and run:
connector.runHint:
Then keep it connected with . The code expires in 10 minutes and is single-use.

Tip: in File Explorer, open the downloads folder, type in the address bar and press Enter to open the terminal there.
connector.noAgents: You don't have any agent linked yet.
connector.namePrompt: Name for this agent (e.g. "Living room PC"):
connector.planRequired: Noctcom Connector unlocks with any paid plan (from €1/month)

disks.title: Storage disks
disks.useDiskHint: “Use this disk” creates a <folder></folder> folder on it to store your encrypted files; it doesn't format or delete anything. Disk formatting is coming in the next version of the agent.
disks.formatTitle: Format the disk (erases all its content)
disks.noServerDisks: No disks detected on the server. Connect a disk (USB/SATA) to the machine running Noctcom and it will appear here to add as a volume.

exportImport.title: Export / Import vault
exportImport.exportDesc: Download your entire vault as an encrypted file. You can import it into any Noctcom instance.
exportImport.importDesc: Import a file exported from another instance.

danger.title: Danger zone
danger.deleteDesc: Permanently deletes your account and all your files. This action is irreversible.
danger.confirmMessage: Your account, all your vaults and all your encrypted files will be deleted forever. There's no way to recover them. This action is irreversible.

profile.metricsPrivacyNote: Aggregate totals only — no trackers, no individual data, like everything else.

empty.title: No recent activity
empty.description: Files you upload will appear here

starred.title: Starred
starred.subtitle: Files and folders you marked as favorites

empty.title: No shared files
empty.description: Files you share will appear here

e2e.title: Share with E2E encryption
e2e.description: Shared files are encrypted with a unique derived key. The recipient needs the full link with the key fragment to decrypt. Noctcom never accesses the content.

trash.title: Trash
trash.subtitle: Files are permanently deleted after 30 days
trash.encryptedNotice: Files in the trash remain encrypted. When deleted permanently, the decryption keys are destroyed.
trash.expiry: {hasDate, select, no {Expires in —} other {{days, plural, =0 {Expiring} =1 {Expires in 1 day} other {Expires in # days}}}}
trash.confirmMessage: «{name}» will be deleted forever, along with its encrypted content. This action cannot be undone.

activity.title: Activity log
activity.subtitle: Encrypted history of actions in your vault

empty.title: No activity
empty.subtitle: The actions you take will appear here, encrypted

shareModal.title: Share file
shareModal.securityNote: The file key is encrypted with the recipient's public key (sealed box). Neither Noctcom nor anyone else can access the contents.
shareModal.noContacts: You don't have any contacts yet. Send a request below; you can only share with contacts who have accepted you.
shareModal.requestHint: The other user must accept your request before you can share files with them.

contacts.title: Contacts
contacts.subtitle: You can only share files with contacts who have accepted you.
contacts.acceptedEmpty: You don't have any contacts yet. Add someone by their username.
contacts.note: Sharing requires mutual consent: nobody can push files to you without your acceptance first. On accept, the contact's public key is pinned.

formatDiskModal.title: Format disk
formatDiskModal.warningTitle: WARNING: This action will ERASE ALL DATA on this disk.
formatDiskModal.toastSuccess: Disk formatted and registered as the active volume

agentFormatModal.toastSuccess: Disk formatted and ready to store
agentFormatModal.title: Format disk
agentFormatModal.warningTitle: WARNING: this will ERASE all the contents of the disk.
agentFormatModal.warningBody: For safety, the agent only formats empty disks and never the system disk. The operation is irreversible.

error.notUploaded: This file was not uploaded correctly. Delete it and upload it again.

largeFile.title: Large file ({size})
largeFile.description: Decrypting this file in memory can be slow and use a lot of RAM.

unsupported.office: Office files cannot be previewed in the browser
unsupported.unknown: This file type has no preview available

toasts.newDevice: New device connected to your account
toasts.offline: Offline — your changes will sync when you're back
toasts.uploadQueued: “{name}” encrypted and queued — it will upload when you're back online
toasts.offlineUploadsSynced: {count, plural, one {# pending file uploaded} other {# pending files uploaded}}
toasts.offlineUploadRejected: A queued upload was rejected by the server: {error}

pwa.installed: Noctcom installed — find it on your home screen
pwa.heroInstall: Download Noctcom as an app on your phone or computer
pwa.heroInstallSub: Straight from your browser, no app stores. Same vault, same encryption.
pwa.heroInstallFallback: In your browser: open the menu and choose “Install app” (Chrome/Edge), or use Safari on iPhone/iPad.

intro.title:
What does the server actually

see?
intro.sub: A step-by-step test of Noctcom's zero-knowledge encryption. No tricks: these are the real algorithms from the open-source code.

test1.name: Test 1 — the password
test1.mkComment: // Master Key: derived and used ONLY on your device
test1.networkTitle: What goes out over the network
test1.note: Neither the password nor the MK appears in the request: a challenge is signed with a key that only exists if you know the password.
test1.verdict: The server never receives your password. It can't even attempt to decrypt you.

test2.name: Test 2 — the file
test2.docLine3: …the intermediary's account and the shell company
test2.note: The AAD binds each block to its position: not even whoever controls the storage can reorder or mix blocks without breaking verification.
test2.verdict: Encryption happens before a single byte leaves your device.

test3.name: Test 3 — the network
test3.keyComment: // the key, wrapped with YOUR key
test3.note: There isn't a single readable field. The server receives sealed envelopes and stores them without being able to open them.
test3.verdict: Only ciphertext crosses the network — even with broken TLS, there'd be nothing to read.

test4.name: Test 4 — the server itself
test4.rowsComment: (2 rows — opaque bytes, no possible key)
test4.query2: SELECT email, password FROM users; -- and the personal data?
test4.errComment: -- only email_hash (BLAKE2b) and an OPAQUE verifier exist
test4.note: This is what the Noctcom operator would see, an attacker who stole the entire database… or anyone with a disclosure order: there is no key on the server with which to decrypt anything.
test4.verdict: Server access to your content: 0 readable bytes.

outro.s1: The password never leaves the device
outro.s4: The server can't read anything — not even if it wanted to

meta.description: Your private vault. Encrypted on your device. No one else can open it. Open source and self-hostable, made by Redder Labs.
